message:
fix(ci): match indented [patch] blocks in fresh-clone regression guard

TOML table headers may have leading whitespace (cargo accepts
`  [patch.crates-io]` and resolves it as an active patch table). The
prior `^\[patch` regex only matched column-0 entries, so an accidentally
indented paste from another config file would bypass the guard.

Widen the check to `^[[:space:]]*\[patch` and apply the same pattern to
the informational awk print step so the two stages agree.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -55,16 +55,17 @@ jobs:
         working-directory: cass
         run: |
           echo "=== [patch] sections in Cargo.toml ==="
-          awk '/^\[patch/,/^$/' Cargo.toml || true
+          awk '/^[[:space:]]*\[patch/,/^[[:space:]]*$/' Cargo.toml || true
 
       - name: Assert no active [patch] blocks for sibling repos
         working-directory: cass
         run: |
           set -euo pipefail
           # Any uncommented [patch."..."] line is a regression risk for #181.
-          # Cargo.toml comments start with `#`, so an active [patch] line
-          # starts at column 0 with `[patch`.
-          if grep -nE '^\[patch' Cargo.toml; then
+          # TOML table headers may have leading whitespace (cargo honors this),
+          # so match `^[[:space:]]*\[patch` rather than `^\[patch`.
+          # Comments start with `#`, so they still won't match this pattern.
+          if grep -nE '^[[:space:]]*\[patch' Cargo.toml; then
             echo ""
             echo "ERROR: Cargo.toml has active [patch] block(s)."
             echo "These point at local sibling paths and break fresh-clone builds (#181)."
